rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the target implementation scope when binding implementation-level details, and into the component type scope for port-level assumptions and guarantees.
Preserve AADL structure by using only ports, subcomponents, and connections explicitly defined in the input model.
generic_rules: Place contracts in the most specific valid scope: component type for port relationships, implementation for implementation-bound bindings.
Only reference features, subcomponents, or data types that are explicitly declared in the AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes use the format: annex agree {** ... **};
Boolean expressions must match the declared data type of referenced ports; do not coerce between numeric and Boolean domains.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as if they are AGREE variables.
Do not use subcomponent paths in component type annexes unless mapped through visible ports.
irrelevant_topics: assume, guarantee, assign, validator, repair, fusion logic, AGREE clause selection
